diagrams.title: Exception Class Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows how user-defined exceptions fit into the C++ standard exception hierarchy, with std::exception as the base class and common standard exceptions like std::runtime_error and std::logic_error, illustrating that custom exceptions should derive from these to leverage polymorphic exception handling.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the inheritance relationship between standard C++ exceptions and user-defined exceptions, showing how to properly structure custom exception classes for effective exception handling.

diagrams.title: Try-Catch-Throw Flow
diagrams.description: Illustrates the control flow when an exception is thrown in a try block and caught by a catch block, showing how the exception object is created at throw point and propagates through the call stack until caught.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the mechanism of throwing and catching user-defined exceptions, helping students understand how exceptions move through the program and are handled.

diagrams.title: User-Defined Exception Class Structure
diagrams.description: Shows a sample custom exception class that inherits from std::runtime_error, demonstrating the typical structure including constructor initialization, custom error message storage, and the required override of the what() method for error description.
diagrams.purpose: Provides a template for creating well-structured user-defined exception classes that follow C++ best practices for exception handling.
